name: Section-aware text parsing utility
description: Regex-based section splitter for astronomy papers. Splits body text into (section_name, start_char, end_char, text) tuples for standard IMRaD sections (Introduction, Methods/Observations, Results, Discussion, Conclusions). Pure Python utility with no DB dependencies.
acceptance: File src/scix/section_parser.py exists and exports a parse_sections() function
parse_sections() returns list of tuples: (section_name, start_char, end_char, text)
Handles standard section headers: Introduction, Methods, Observations, Results, Discussion, Conclusions
Returns single ('full', 0, len, text) tuple for papers without recognizable section headers
Tests in tests/test_section_parser.py cover: standard IMRaD paper, no-section paper, mixed case headers, numbered sections (e.g. '1. Introduction'), subsections
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_section_parser.py -v' passes with 0 failures

name: Citation contexts migration
description: SQL migration 011 creating the citation_contexts table with columns: id (serial PK), source_bibcode (text, not null), target_bibcode (text, not null), context_text (text, not null), char_offset (integer), intent (text, nullable for later classification). Includes indexes on (source_bibcode, target_bibcode) and on target_bibcode. No FK constraints (same pattern as citation_edges).
acceptance: File migrations/011_citation_contexts.sql exists
Migration creates citation_contexts table with columns: id serial PK, source_bibcode text NOT NULL, target_bibcode text NOT NULL, context_text text NOT NULL, char_offset integer, intent text
Migration creates composite index on (source_bibcode, target_bibcode)
Migration creates index on target_bibcode
Migration is wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT

Running 'python -c "import pathlib; sql=pathlib.Path(\"migrations/011_citation_contexts.sql\").read_text(); assert \"citation_contexts\" in sql; assert \"BEGIN\" in sql; assert \"COMMIT\" in sql; print(\"OK\")"' prints OK

name: Coverage bias analysis script and report
description: Python script scripts/coverage_bias_analysis.py that queries the DB to compare distributions between full-text (body IS NOT NULL) and abstract-only papers across: arxiv_class, year, citation_count, and journal (pub). Outputs a markdown report to docs/full_text_coverage_analysis.md with tables showing distribution comparisons. Uses matplotlib for chart generation saved as PNGs in docs/figures/. Script should be runnable standalone but also importable for testing.
acceptance: File scripts/coverage_bias_analysis.py exists and is executable
Script imports cleanly: python -c 'import importlib.util; spec=importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("m","scripts/coverage_bias_analysis.py"); mod=importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)'
Script has functions: get_year_distribution(), get_arxiv_distribution(), get_citation_distribution(), get_journal_distribution(), generate_report()
generate_report() produces markdown with distribution comparison tables
Tests in tests/test_coverage_bias.py mock DB queries and verify report generation
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_coverage_bias.py -v' passes with 0 failures

name: Citation context extraction pipeline
description: Pipeline in src/scix/citation_context.py that: (1) extracts ~250-word context windows around inline citation markers [N] in body text, (2) resolves numbered [N] markers to target bibcodes via the paper's reference[] array (stored in raw JSONB -> 'reference' key), (3) stores results in citation_contexts table. Includes batch processing with COPY for performance. Uses the section_parser for context enrichment (noting which section a citation appears in).
acceptance: File src/scix/citation_context.py exists and exports: extract_citation_contexts(), resolve_citation_markers(), process_paper(), run_pipeline()
extract_citation_contexts() finds [N] patterns in text and returns ~250-word context windows with char offsets
resolve_citation_markers() maps [N] to bibcodes using a reference list (position N-1 in array)
process_paper() combines extraction and resolution for a single paper
run_pipeline() processes papers from DB in batches, writing to citation_contexts table
Tests in tests/test_citation_context.py cover: single [1] marker, multiple [1,2,3] markers, author-year style (graceful skip), edge cases (marker at start/end of text, N > len(references))
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_citation_context.py -v' passes with 0 failures

name: read_paper_section and search_within_paper MCP tools
description: Add two new MCP tools to src/scix/mcp_server.py: (1) read_paper_section(bibcode, section, char_offset=0, limit=5000) - returns section text using section_parser, with has_body flag, falls back to abstract; (2) search_within_paper(bibcode, query) - uses PostgreSQL ts_headline on the body column to find matching passages with 200-char context. Also add corresponding functions to src/scix/search.py. Register tools in list_tools() and _dispatch_tool().
acceptance: search.py exports read_paper_section() and search_within_paper() functions
read_paper_section() returns SearchResult with paper section text, has_body flag, and pagination support
search_within_paper() returns SearchResult with matching passages using ts_headline
mcp_server.py registers both tools in list_tools() with correct inputSchema
mcp_server.py dispatches both tools in _dispatch_tool()
Tests cover: section retrieval for paper with body, fallback to abstract for paper without body, pagination, search_within_paper with matching/non-matching queries
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_mcp_paper_tools.py -v' passes with 0 failures

name: Citation intent classification pipeline
description: Pipeline in src/scix/citation_intent.py that classifies citation contexts into 3 classes: background, method, result_comparison. Supports two backends: (1) local SciBERT/transformer model fine-tuned on SciCite dataset, (2) LLM classification via Anthropic API as fallback. Updates the intent column in citation_contexts table. Includes training script for the SciBERT model.
acceptance: File src/scix/citation_intent.py exists and exports: classify_intent(), classify_batch(), IntentClassifier protocol, SciBertClassifier class, LLMClassifier class
classify_intent() accepts context_text and returns one of: 'background', 'method', 'result_comparison'
classify_batch() processes multiple contexts efficiently (batched model inference or batched API calls)
SciBertClassifier wraps a transformers pipeline for local inference
LLMClassifier provides Anthropic API fallback classification
File scripts/train_citation_intent.py exists with training loop for SciCite dataset
Tests in tests/test_citation_intent.py mock the model and test classification logic, label validation, batch processing
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_citation_intent.py -v' passes with 0 failures

name: get_citation_context MCP tool
description: Add get_citation_context(source_bibcode, target_bibcode) MCP tool to mcp_server.py. Returns the context text and intent label for why source cites target from the citation_contexts table. Returns null gracefully for pairs without context. Add corresponding function to search.py.
acceptance: search.py exports get_citation_context() function that queries citation_contexts table
get_citation_context() returns SearchResult with context_text, intent, char_offset for matching pairs
Returns empty result (not error) for pairs without context
mcp_server.py registers get_citation_context tool in list_tools() with source_bibcode and target_bibcode as required params
mcp_server.py dispatches get_citation_context in _dispatch_tool()
Tests cover: existing citation pair returns context, non-existent pair returns empty, multiple contexts for same pair
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_mcp_citation_context.py -v' passes with 0 failures
